SEARCH
Package org.openapitools.codegen.meta.features

Enum DataTypeFeature

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<DataTypeFeature>
org.openapitools.codegen.meta.features.DataTypeFeature
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<DataTypeFeature>, java.lang.constant.Constable
public enum DataTypeFeature extends Enum<DataTypeFeature>
Defines common data types supported by a generator. Some of these features are defined in specs, and some are specific to the tool. Where data types are listed as tool-specific, this either indicates that the data type is common enough that it is an officially supported custom data type by the toolset (see Decimal), or that the consideration of a special type isn't explicitly mentioned by the specification(s) but differs enough across languages that it warrants a special callout (see ArrayOfModel).

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class java.lang.Enum

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    AnyType
    When no type is defined, any data type is accepted
    Array
    Supports arrays of data
    ArrayOfCollectionOfEnum
    Supports an array of arrays (enums)
    ArrayOfCollectionOfModel
    Supports an array of arrays (models)
    ArrayOfCollectionOfPrimitives
    Supports an array of arrays (primitives)
    ArrayOfEnum
    Supports an array of enum
    ArrayOfModel
    Supports an array of models
    Binary
    Supports string/binary: any collection of octets
    Boolean
    Supports boolean
    Byte
    Supports string/byte: base64 encoded
    CollectionFormat
    Supports specifying the format of the array if type array is used (one of: csv, ssv, tsv, pipes).
    CollectionFormatMulti
    Supports collection format=multi.
    Custom
    Supports a generator-specific support usually via type=string's format property (e.g.
    Date
    Supports string/date: full-date RFC3339
    DateTime
    Supports string/date-time: date-time RFC3339
    Decimal
    Supports number/decimal (a special case for some languages)
    Double
    Supports number/double
    Enum
    Supports enum properties
    File
    Supports file inputs (e.g.
    Float
    Supports number/float
    Int32
    Supports integer/int32
    Int64
    Supports integer/int64
    MapOfCollectionOfEnum
    Supports a map of arrays (enums)
    MapOfCollectionOfModel
    Supports a map of arrays (models)
    MapOfCollectionOfPrimitives
    Supports a map of arrays (primitives)
    MapOfEnum
    Supports a map of enums
    MapOfModel
    Supports a map of models
    Maps
    Supports map of data
    Null
    A JSON "null" value added in openapi v3.1.0
    Object
    An unordered set of properties mapping a string to an instance
    Password
    Supports string/password: A hint to UIs to obscure input.
    String
    Supports string
    Uuid
    String uuid data

  • Enum Constant Details

    • Custom

      public static final DataTypeFeature Custom
      Supports a generator-specific support usually via type=string's format property (e.g. email, uuid, etc), should be documented in generator README.

      Loosely described in OpenAPI Specification(s). Generally means a custom "format" option applied to a string-typed property.

    • Int32

      public static final DataTypeFeature Int32
      Supports integer/int32

    • Int64

      public static final DataTypeFeature Int64
      Supports integer/int64

    • Float

      public static final DataTypeFeature Float
      Supports number/float

    • Double

      public static final DataTypeFeature Double
      Supports number/double

    • Decimal

      public static final DataTypeFeature Decimal
      Supports number/decimal (a special case for some languages)

      Decimal is not a type defined by OAS 2.0 specification

    • String

      public static final DataTypeFeature String
      Supports string

    • Byte

      public static final DataTypeFeature Byte
      Supports string/byte: base64 encoded

    • Binary

      public static final DataTypeFeature Binary
      Supports string/binary: any collection of octets

    • Boolean

      public static final DataTypeFeature Boolean
      Supports boolean

    • Date

      public static final DataTypeFeature Date
      Supports string/date: full-date RFC3339
      See Also:
      • RFC3339

    • DateTime

      public static final DataTypeFeature DateTime
      Supports string/date-time: date-time RFC3339
      See Also:
      • RFC3339

    • Password

      public static final DataTypeFeature Password
      Supports string/password: A hint to UIs to obscure input.

      This should be used as an indicator for password best practices, such as assigning a variable to a character array rather than string, avoiding logging the variable in clear text, and masking the value in any user inputs. See OWASP for best practices.

    • File

      public static final DataTypeFeature File
      Supports file inputs (e.g. multipart support).

      OAS 3.x defines files differently.

      OAS 3.x does not have an explicit "file" type and instead relies on ContentType or response types. That's not to say a generator doesn't support files, only that there's no direct "file" type defined in the spec document.

      NOTE: The default workflow may provide an "isFile" helper or synthesize the assumptions around files in the case of OAS 3.x.

    • Uuid

      public static final DataTypeFeature Uuid
      String uuid data

    • Array

      public static final DataTypeFeature Array
      Supports arrays of data

    • Null

      public static final DataTypeFeature Null
      A JSON "null" value added in openapi v3.1.0

    • AnyType

      public static final DataTypeFeature AnyType
      When no type is defined, any data type is accepted

    • Object

      public static final DataTypeFeature Object
      An unordered set of properties mapping a string to an instance

    • Maps

      public static final DataTypeFeature Maps
      Supports map of data

    • CollectionFormat

      public static final DataTypeFeature CollectionFormat
      Supports specifying the format of the array if type array is used (one of: csv, ssv, tsv, pipes).

      For multi support, check CollectionFormatMulti. OAS 3.x removes collectionFormat in favor of Style properties.

    • CollectionFormatMulti

      public static final DataTypeFeature CollectionFormatMulti
      Supports collection format=multi.

      This is special cased because it is not as easily implemented as a delimiter as with CollectionFormat. OAS 3.x removes collectionFormat for style properties.

    • Enum

      public static final DataTypeFeature Enum
      Supports enum properties

    • ArrayOfEnum

      public static final DataTypeFeature ArrayOfEnum
      Supports an array of enum

    • ArrayOfModel

      public static final DataTypeFeature ArrayOfModel
      Supports an array of models

    • ArrayOfCollectionOfPrimitives

      public static final DataTypeFeature ArrayOfCollectionOfPrimitives
      Supports an array of arrays (primitives)

    • ArrayOfCollectionOfModel

      public static final DataTypeFeature ArrayOfCollectionOfModel
      Supports an array of arrays (models)

    • ArrayOfCollectionOfEnum

      public static final DataTypeFeature ArrayOfCollectionOfEnum
      Supports an array of arrays (enums)

    • MapOfEnum

      public static final DataTypeFeature MapOfEnum
      Supports a map of enums

    • MapOfModel

      public static final DataTypeFeature MapOfModel
      Supports a map of models

    • MapOfCollectionOfPrimitives

      public static final DataTypeFeature MapOfCollectionOfPrimitives
      Supports a map of arrays (primitives)

    • MapOfCollectionOfModel

      public static final DataTypeFeature MapOfCollectionOfModel
      Supports a map of arrays (models)

    • MapOfCollectionOfEnum

      public static final DataTypeFeature MapOfCollectionOfEnum
      Supports a map of arrays (enums)

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static DataTypeFeature[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum type, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static DataTypeFeature valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this type with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this type. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum type has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null
